regulatory_references.name: ISO 27001
regulatory_references.relevance: Required for cloud and data-related transformation projects

regulatory_references.name: TOGAF
regulatory_references.relevance: Enterprise architecture standard for transformation programs

regulatory_references.name: COBIT
regulatory_references.full_name: Control Objectives for Information and Related Technologies
regulatory_references.relevance: IT governance framework for digital transformation oversight

compliance_patterns.description: Digital transformation projects must address data classification, sovereignty, and protection
Cloud adoption must comply with local data residency and security requirements

evaluator_insights.insight: Digital transformation RFPs prioritize proven delivery capability over theoretical frameworks
evaluator_insights.implication: Proposals must include concrete case studies with measurable outcomes
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Lead with transformation metrics: cost reduction %, process automation rate, user adoption rate

evaluator_insights.insight: Evaluators look for change management integration within technical approaches
evaluator_insights.implication: Pure technology proposals without organizational change strategy score lower
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Dedicate a methodology phase to change management and user adoption

evaluator_insights.insight: Phased delivery with quick wins is preferred over big-bang approaches
evaluator_insights.implication: Methodology must show value delivery within first 3-6 months
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Include an early-value phase with defined quick-win deliverables

methodology_patterns.when_to_use: Enterprise architecture assessment and target state design
ERP and enterprise system implementation projects
Iterative digital product development and platform delivery
IT service management design and transformation

forbidden_assumptions: Do NOT assume the client has an existing enterprise architecture — assess maturity first
Do NOT assume cloud-first is always the right strategy — assess workload suitability
Do NOT conflate digital transformation with IT infrastructure upgrade — transformation includes people and process
